description_long: This bungalow features a robust stone and wood construction with a prominent cross-gabled roof made of gray shingles. The front facade is characterized by a symmetrical arrangement of windows, with three vertically aligned sets on the main gable and additional windows on the side extension. The structure includes a covered porch area supported by wooden posts, and the exterior is surrounded by a low stone wall and lush green landscaping. The roofline is complex, with intersecting gables and a secondary wing extending to one side, topped with a lighter wood-shingled roof. The windows are multi-paned and framed in white, providing a classic residential appearance. The property is set on a rectangular grassy plot with shrubbery and a paved walkway leading to the entrance.
description: A stone and wood bungalow with a cross-gabled gray shingle roof, symmetrical front windows, a covered porch, and lush landscaping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable with three vertically stacked windows, the covered porch to the left, and the stone wall enclosing the grassy yard.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the intersecting gabled roof, the extension with a lighter wood roof, and dense greenery along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the long side of the bungalow, multiple windows, the paved walkway, and the full extent of the porch and landscaping.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the complex roof structure, the porch extension, and the stone boundary wall.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
